Verified via web search: HL7 version 2.4 is a real standard released in 2000, widely used in healthcare systems. https://community.intersystems.com/post/types-hl7-adt-message-and-example-adta04
Verified via web search: ADT^A04 is the official HL7 message type for patient registration (Pre-admit). https://hl7-definition.caristix.com/v2/HL7v2.3/TriggerEvents/ADT_A04

Verified via web search: These are the standard HL7 2.x encoding characters (component separator ^, repetition separator ~, escape character \, subcomponent separator &). https://rhapsody.health/resources/hl7-encoding-characters/
Verified via code analysis: MedinTux is the healthcare management system used throughout the environment. This is the correct sending application name.

privileged_info_summary: The task generates HL7 v2.4 ADT^A04 messages from a synthetic database containing three test patients: MOREAU Claire (DOB 1987-04-22, Female, Paris, NIR 2870475...), BERNARD Philippe (DOB 1955-11-03, Male, Lyon, NIR 1551169...), and PETIT Isabelle (DOB 2001-08-15, Female, Marseille, NIR 2010813...). The output file must be saved to /home/ga/hl7_output/adt_messages.hl7. All patient identifiers and demographic data are exactly as specified in the metadata and are dynamically inserted during task setup. The HL7 message type ADT^A04 (Register a Patient) and version 2.4 specifications are standard healthcare messaging formats with encoding characters ^~\& being the default HL7 delimiters.
